steps.title: Identify the transformation rules for shifts
steps.content:
When a graph is translated:
  • Right by $h$ units: replace $x$ with $(x - h)$
  • Down by $k$ units: subtract $k$ from the function value
Since the graph moves 4 units right and 3 units down, we need to apply both rules to $f(x)$.

steps.title: Apply the rightward shift of 4 units
steps.content:
Replace $x$ with $(x - 4)$ in the original function:
$$f(x - 4) = \frac{a-19}{x-4} + 5$$

steps.title: Apply the downward shift of 3 units
steps.content:
Subtract 3 from the result:
$$g(x) = \frac{a-19}{x-4} + 5 - 3 = \frac{a-19}{x-4} + 2$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
